cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure out when Richard could have gone to the bakery. The bakery is closed after 7pm, so he has to go before that. First, let's list out all the times Richard was seen doing other things. - Richard woke up at 8am. So he's awake starting at 8am. - From 9am to 2pm, Betty saw him at the library reading. That's a solid block of time he was at the library. - Then David saw him at the electronic store from 2pm to 4pm, fixing a computer. So that's another block, right after the library. - Mary saw him taking photos near the Eiffel Tower from 4pm to 5pm. So after the electronic store, he went there. - Then Hannah saw him on a rooftop from 5pm to 6pm. So that follows the photo session. - Susan saw him driving to the water park from 6pm to 7pm. So that's the next activity. Now, the bakery is closed after 7pm, so he must have gone before 7pm. But the question is, when was he not occupied? Let's check each time frame. From 8am (he woke up) to 9am, there's a gap. The first known activity starts at 9am. So between 8am and 9am, he wasn't seen doing anything. That could be a possible time for the bakery. Then, from 9am to 2pm, he was at the library. So he can't go to the bakery during that time. From 2pm to 4pm, he was at the electronic store. So no bakery there either. 4pm to 5pm: photos at Eiffel Tower. Still occupied. 5pm to 6pm: rooftop. Still occupied. 6pm to 7pm: driving to water park. During that time, he's driving, so probably not stopping at a bakery. Plus, the bakery is still open until 7pm, but he's already en route to the water park. Unless the bakery is on the way, but the problem doesn't say that. We have to assume the activities are separate. So, the only time window where he isn't accounted for is between 8am and 9am. That's when he just woke up and before he went to the library. So the answer must be option D: 8am to 9am. Let me check the options again: A) 6pm to 7pm – he was driving to the water
